topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the old park bench felt suddenly cold beneath my fingers. I’d been sketching under the oak tree, lost in the lines, when I spotted it—a thick, dark wallet lying half-hidden in the damp grass. My heart gave a sudden, heavy thump against my ribs. As I cautiously picked it up, the weight of the contents settled in my palm, a startling, undeniable bulk. Peering inside, the sheer volume of bills made my breath hitch. A wave of intense unease washed over me; this wasn't just found money; this felt like a trap. I quickly shoved it deep into my backpack, my movements jerky, and I didn't wait to see who owned it. I just wanted to be gone.

[story 2]

It was late, the streetlights casting long, skeletal shadows across the alleyway. I was taking a shortcut home, and then I saw it—a leather item dropped near a overflowing dumpster. When I retrieved it, my hands started trembling almost immediately. The wallet was heavy, and when I flipped it open, the sight of the stacks of cash made my stomach clench tight. Every nerve ending seemed to be screaming. I looked left, then right, half-expecting someone to materialize from the darkness. I mumbled something incoherent, shoved the wallet into my coat pocket with frantic haste, and practically bolted, the sound of my own quickened breathing echoing in the deserted space.

[story 3]

The discovery happened entirely by accident. I was browsing a donation box at the community center, and there, tucked beneath some crumpled flyers, was a wallet. My gaze dropped to the interior, and the reality of the cash inside hit me like a physical blow. A sudden, icy sensation gripped my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I stared at the money, my eyes wide and unblinking. A prickling sensation ran down my arms. I felt a desperate need to dispose of the object immediately. Without a word, I slipped it into the nearest bag, my fingers clumsy as I tried to secure the clasp, my entire focus narrowed down to the need to escape the vicinity.
